items.title: Sources: Chargers, TE Njoku agree to 1-yr. deal
items.body_text: After nine years with the Browns, David Njoku has agreed to terms with the Chargers on a one-year deal worth up to $8 million, sources told NFL Network's Ian Rapoport.

items.title: Harbaugh-led Giants to host Cowboys in Week 1
items.body_text: The Giants will host the Cowboys on Sept. 13 in the first "Sunday Night Football" game of the 2026 season.

items.title: Klubnik unshaken by fall in draft: 'I'm a winner'
items.body_text: Jets rookie QB Cade Klubnik said he's still "a winner" despite a difficult final season at Clemson that saw his draft stock fall.
items.author: Rich Cimini

items.title: Harbaugh: Decision on OBJ could wait until camp
items.body_text: Giants coach John Harbaugh said he spoke to Odell Beckham Jr. three to four times in the past week as they remain in touch about reuniting. But they will "play it out over the next month and into training camp" before making a decision.
items.author: Jordan Raanan

items.title: Payton: Nix 'doing good,' on course for minicamp
items.body_text: Broncos coach Sean Payton said Saturday that quarterback Bo Nix is on track to participate on a limited basis in the team's minicamp next month and will be "full speed'' by the time the team's training camp begins this summer.
items.author: Jeff Legwold

items.title: Jets give RB Hall 3-year, $45.75M extension
items.body_text: Jets running back Breece Hall, given the franchise tag on March 3, agreed to a three-year, $43.5 million contract Friday that can be worth up to $45.75 million, sources told ESPN.
items.author: Rich Cimini

items.title: Ex-NFLer Wint added to White's Contender Series
items.body_text: Former NFL linebacker Anthony Wint, who played briefly for the New York Jets in 2018, will fight in Dana White's Contender Series later this year in an effort to land a UFC contract.
items.author: Brett Okamoto

items.title: Sources: Seahawks interest 'soft' from buyers
items.body_text: The Seahawks haven't generated as much buyer interest as NFL officials originally expected, sources told ESPN.
